summary: Marked melee/it/items/itcoin.c as matching in the build configuration. The PR body states the file was already matched but had not yet been marked that way, so the change switches it from NonMatching/asm output to Matching/src object output.
what_changed: In configure.py, changed Object(NonMatching, "melee/it/items/itcoin.c") to Object(Matching, "melee/it/items/itcoin.c").
In obj_files.mk, changed the itcoin object path from $(BUILD_DIR)/asm/melee/it/items/itcoin.s.o to $(BUILD_DIR)/src/melee/it/items/itcoin.c.o.
No source code changes to itcoin.c are shown in the PR slice; this was a metadata/build-list update for an already matched file.
smart_moves: Kept configure.py and obj_files.mk in sync: marking the object as Matching in the Python build config also required switching the Makefile object from asm to src.
Used a minimal two-line change to reflect matching status without touching unrelated item objects.
Preserved neighboring item object ordering around itmasterhandlaser, itmasterhandbullet, itcrazyhandbomb, itkirby_2F23, it_2F28, and it_2F2B.
decomp_lessons: When a file is already functionally matched, remember to update both the high-level object classification and the generated/manual object list so the build consumes the C object instead of the asm object.
For matching-status PRs, the important evidence is often the NonMatching-to-Matching transition plus the asm .s.o-to-src .c.o transition, not a source diff.
Small bookkeeping PRs can still matter because leaving a matched file listed as NonMatching prevents it from contributing to project-wide matching progress.
naming_conventions: Item source files under melee/it/items use lowercase names such as itcoin.c.
Matching status in configure.py is represented with Object(Matching, "path/to/file.c") versus Object(NonMatching, "path/to/file.c").
obj_files.mk distinguishes nonmatching assembly objects under $(BUILD_DIR)/asm/.../*.s.o from matching C objects under $(BUILD_DIR)/src/.../*.c.o.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Promoted itcoin from assembly-backed object output to C-backed object output by replacing asm/melee/it/items/itcoin.s.o with src/melee/it/items/itcoin.c.o.
The PR did not include matching tactics for code generation, regalloc, fakematches, or symbol changes; evidence only shows the final status flip.
follow_up_queries: Find other PRs that only switch Object(NonMatching) to Object(Matching) and update obj_files.mk from asm to src.
Search for remaining melee/it/items files still listed as NonMatching in configure.py.
Check whether obj_files.mk is generated from configure.py or manually maintained for matching-status changes.
